rationale: Both engage the ground-truth threads (regression tests, tag enum) and both flag the level-2 NotImplementedError guard as over-strict, hitting the latent gap. Y's version is far more concrete and verifiable: it names five specific pre-existing tests with exact line numbers that call sleep(level=2)+wake_up() and will now raise, backed by cited grep evidence — this is essentially the exact CI-breaking scenario from issue #4905 that both human reviewers missed. X's analogous finding (default level=2 'bricks' the engine) is a plausible but less-verified inference, and X carries a couple of speculative/self-admitted-no-impact points (allocator 'default' tag, pre-existing desync) that dilute precision. Y's other findings are all tightly grounded with per-item evidence trails, giving it the edge on precision and actionability despite a slightly narrower recall of the enum-suggestion thread.
